content.problemStatement: Rectify the error in the code.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string name consisting of visible characters without spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the character at index 7 (0-indexed) of the given string. If the string length is less than 8, print an empty line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The string "CodeChef" has length 8, so the character at index 7 is 'f'.

solution.approach: The key insight is to directly access the character at index 7 of the input string. If the string length is less than 8, print an empty line. This approach works because string indices in most programming languages are 0-based, meaning the first character is at index 0.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total. No precomputation is needed.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the dominant structure is the input string itself, which does not depend on the algorithm but on the input size.
